key_insight: AI avatar videos can deliver scalable tobacco-prevention messages on social media, but this trial did not show they outperformed a written fact sheet.
clinical_relevance: For youth-facing digital prevention, generative AI avatars may be a practical content format, though current evidence supports feasibility more than superior behavior-change impact.
method: Three-arm randomized online intervention study with pre-post assessment
Participants aged 16+ were assigned to one of two AI avatar video messages or a written American Cancer Society fact sheet
Primary outcome: post-intervention intention to avoid smoking and secondhand smoke, adjusted for baseline intention
evidence_signal: Among 400 randomized participants, 272 were included in the complete baseline-adjusted analysis; intention improved in all groups, with no statistically significant between-group differences.
watch_item: The study evaluated short-term intention rather than actual smoking behavior, so real-world prevention impact remains uncertain.
paper_title: Generative AI avatar videos for tobacco prevention on social media: a randomized controlled trial
